raw_cv_text:
Nadia Nomad
mperial College London – MEng Computing (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) October 2022 – Present
• Year 2: First Class Honours
• Year 1: Upper Second Class Honours
• Key Modules: Data Structures and Algorithms, Databases, Compilers, Computer Architecture, Operating Systems,
Programming (C, Java, Kotlin, Haskell, Scala).
Sishya – Higher Secondary Education, India May 2020 – June 2022
• Achieved 98.5% overall.
• Top Scores: 100% in Mathematics, 99% in Computer Science, 98% in Physics, and 94% in Chemistry.
WORK EXPERIENCE
Software Developer – Dotplot January 2023 – Present
• Spearheaded the development of an at-home breast health monitoring system.
o Created a patented core functionality that accurately tracks user interactions on a torso rendering.
• Designed and implemented both frontend (SwiftUI, AVFoundation, Vision, MetalKit) and backend systems.
o Transitioned from using Python’s FastAPI framework on an Azure Virtual Machine to a serverless
architecture using Azure App Functions.
• Innovated GPU programming and graphics rendering techniques using Metal. This impacted the product’s
performance, such as using up less battery and preventing overheating.
Cherry Hill Tuitions November 2022 – January 2023
• Tutored students across all age groups in the subjects of Mathematics, Science, and English.
• Main challenge: Hold the interest of students while delivering the required content.
PROJECTS
TransitWatch (Swift) 2024
• Developed an iOS app that helps London commuters avoid delays caused by public transport by providing real-
time updates, alternate routes and departure reminders based on their schedule in their iCloud calendar.
• Applied the principles of Human-Centered Development, iterating the app based on user feedback to enhance
user experience and functionality.
WACC Compiler (Scala) 2024
• Engineered a compiler for a made up language in Scala, to compile into x86 AT&T syntax assembly.
• The Parsley parser combinator library is used to convert high-level code into the internal representation (three
address code). Symbol tables are generated for efficient variable to register mapping and scoping.
• Extended functionality to include class and object creation, alongside robust runtime error handling.
PintOS 2023 – 2024
• Iterated on a simple operating system, with a focus on process scheduling, user program management, and virtual
memory implementation.
• Gained deep insight into low-level OS design, concurrency for single-core processors, and programming in C.
T.M. Muhammad Mohsin
London, United Kingdom
+44 77788 32565
muhammadmohsin0404@gmail.com
x86 Emulator and Assembler 2023
• Developed an x86 emulator and assembler in C, demonstrating proficiency in low-level programming.
• Extended the project with a Quizlet-like application capable of querying Wikipedia, showcasing adaptability and
innovation in software development.
Medical Transcription Software 2019 – 2022
• Developed an AI-powered medical transcription software capable of analyzing voice input to identify and
document medical issues.
• Applied Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques and implemented machine learning algorithms.
Reverse Backdoor Trojan 2020
• Engineered a sophisticated reverse backdoor trojan capable of bypassing detection by leading antivirus software,
including Symantec.
• Innovatively used GitHub Gists for covert communication with the hacker machine, effectively evading firewalls.
Automated Water Pump with Mobile App | School's Science Fest 2019
• Designed and developed an app-controlled automated water pump system that uses real-time water level data to
control pump activation and deactivation.
• Implemented manual controls and monitoring via a mobile app for ease of access.
Digital Clinometer | ICSE Board Math project 2019
• Created a device to measure the height and distance of any large object, by the press of a button.
• Automates trigonometric calculations typically done by hand for traditional clinometers.
Guiding Car for Blind People | School Fest ‘Jubilaire’ 2018
• Developed a car designed to assist blind individuals in navigating complex environments, such as a maze.
• Built an app for this car that would communicate information about obstacles through vibrations.
ACHIEVEMENTS AND AWARDS
Model United Nations: Placed 3rd in the MUN held at Chettinad Vidyashram School, Chennai, India. 2020
Robotics Event: Secured 1st place in the inter-house robotics competition at the school science fest. 2019
Athletics: Won 1st place in the Medley event at the school's annual sports meet. 2019
IT PowerPoint Event: Awarded 1st place in the IT PowerPoint competition at the ‘Avsar’ school fest. 2018
Olympiads: School topper in National Science and Cyber Olympiads, International Mathematics and English Olympiads.
COURSES
Certified in Cybersecurity (ISC2) August 2023 – Present
CS50’s Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Python (Harvard) January 2021
Learn Ethical Hacking from Scratch (Udemy) January 2020
Build real world application using Python (Udemy) October 2018
Using Python for Research (Harvard) November 2017
Introduction to Python: Fundamentals (Microsoft) September 2017
CS50: Introduction to Computer Science (Harvard) September 2016
SKILLS
Programming Languages: Swift, Python, Java, C, Kotlin, JavaScript, Haskell, Scala.
Platforms: Microsoft Azure, MacOS, iOS, Windows, Linux, Android.
Soft Skills: Curiosity, Teamwork and Leadership, Problem Solving, Initiative, Communication, Attention to Detail.
